ac_validation.text: Given the FastapiPlugin class, when instantiated, then plugin.name returns 'fastapi'.

Given a valid ProjectSpec with backend_id='fastapi' and default config, when files() is called, then the returned list at minimum contains GeneratedFile entries for app/__init__.py, app/main.py, and requirements.txt.
issues: 'at minimum contains' is slightly ambiguous — the test should use exact set membership assertions for these 3 paths.
'default config' is implicitly defined by AC-11 (config={'fastapi': {}}) with internal defaults: orm='sqlalchemy', auth=False, include_alembic=False.
Test must construct ProjectSpec with config={'fastapi': {}} or rely on spec.config.get('fastapi', {}) returning {} from config={}. Both work, but test should be explicit.
ac_validation.suggested_fix: Replace 'at minimum contains' with 'contains' to make the assertion type unambiguous.

ac_validation.text: Given a valid ProjectSpec with backend_id='fastapi', when PluginExecutionEngine.run() is called with a MockTransaction, then files are staged via txn.stage_file() for the core files listed in AC-2a, and dependencies are appended to txn.requirements.
issues: Combines FileProvider AND DependencyProvider assertions in one AC. Failure clarity would benefit from splitting into 2b_i (stage_file assertions) and 2b_ii (requirements assertions).
Use of PluginExecutionEngine with MockTransaction is well-established pattern from T-006/T-007 tests.
ac_validation.suggested_fix: Consider splitting into 2b_i and 2b_ii for failure isolation clarity.

ac_validation.text: Given the plugin's Configurable mixin, when questions() is called, then it returns Question objects whose .key attributes include 'orm', 'auth', 'include_alembic'. The orm question must be QuestionType.CHOICE with options containing ['sqlalchemy', 'none']; auth and include_alembic must be QuestionType.BOOLEAN.
issues: Question order is not specified. Test should use any() pattern, not positional indexing.

ac_validation.text: Given orm='sqlalchemy' in the plugin's config (accessed via spec.config.get('fastapi', {})), when files() is called, then the generated requirements.txt content includes sqlalchemy.
issues: Parenthetical about config access pattern is implementation guidance, not assertion. Already covered by Design Note 1.
ac_validation.suggested_fix: Move config access pattern note to Design Notes (already in DN1).

ac_validation.text: Given include_alembic=True in config, when directories() is called, then 'alembic/' is in the returned list.
Given the FastAPI plugin, when dependencies() is called, then it returns ['fastapi>=0.115', 'uvicorn[standard]>=0.34'].
issues: AC does not specify the config context. With dependencies(self, spec: ProjectSpec), the test must provide a spec. Default config (auth=False/absent) produces the base set — this AC only covers the base case.
ac_validation.suggested_fix: Explicitly state 'with default config (auth absent or False)' to match Given clause.

ac_validation.text: Given a valid ProjectSpec and target directory, when generate() is called with a mock ProcessExecutor, then executor.run() is called with a command list containing ['uv', 'add', 'fastapi>=0.115', 'uvicorn[standard]>=0.34'].
issues: 'containing' is slightly ambiguous (subset vs exact match). Context suggests the command list argument includes exactly these elements, potentially among others.

API spec line 47 shows `executor: ProcessExecutor` — this WOULD trigger the AC-4 scanner if the plugin imports ProcessExecutor. Design Note 7 correctly says to use untyped `executor` or `Any`. The API spec annotation contradicts Design Note 7 (see architecture_readiness).
Mock ProcessExecutor via MagicMock — well-established pattern.
ac_validation.suggested_fix: Clarify to: 'executor.run() was called with a command list that includes these elements as a subsequence.' Also fix API spec to match Design Note 7: use `executor: Any` or untyped.

ac_validation.text: Given include_alembic=False in config, when directories() is called, then 'alembic/' is NOT in the returned list.
Given orm='none' in config, when files() is called, then the generated requirements.txt does NOT include 'sqlalchemy'.

Given an invalid orm value (e.g., 'invalid') in plugin config, when ValidationEngine.validate_plugin_config() is called with the plugin's questions (constructed inline per Design Note 8), then a ValidationError with severity 'error' is returned (matching existing pattern at test_validation.py:test_choice_invalid_option).
issues: Existing pattern at test_validation.py:226 checks `len(errors) >= 1` and `any(e.field == 'db' for e in errors)`. AC-10 additionally requires `severity == 'error'` — ValidationEngine.validate_plugin_config() always emits 'error' for CHOICE violations (line 155 in validation.py).
Test constructs inline Question objects matching the plugin's orm question spec — avoids needing the plugin to exist at test time. Good test-first design.

ac_validation.text: Given config={'fastapi': {}} (empty config dict — plugin uses internal defaults), when files() and directories() are called, then: (a) requirements.txt contains 'sqlalchemy', (b) 'alembic/' is NOT in the returned directories.
issues: Implicitly defines internal defaults: orm defaults to 'sqlalchemy', include_alembic defaults to False. These defaults are NOT documented in the API spec's questions() method.
make_spec() produces config={}. Test must construct custom spec with config={'fastapi': {}} — straightforward dataclass reassignment.
ac_validation.suggested_fix: Document internal defaults in the API spec's questions() method or a Design Note.

ac_validation.text: Given config={} (no 'fastapi' key in ProjectSpec.config), when files() or directories() is called, then no exception is raised and default values are used (the plugin accesses config via spec.config.get('fastapi', {})).
issues: Parenthetical about .get() is implementation guidance, not assertion. Already in Design Note 1.
make_spec() produces config={} by default — compatible with this AC out of the box.
ac_validation.suggested_fix: Move .get() guidance to Design Notes (already in DN1).

ac_validation.text: Given auth=True in config, when dependencies() is called, then the returned list includes 'python-jose[cryptography]>=3.3' and 'passlib[bcrypt]>=1.7'.
issues: Previously BLOCKING due to DependencyProvider.dependencies() lacking spec parameter. NOW FIXED: base.py:49-51 has `dependencies(self, spec: ProjectSpec)`, plugin_execution_engine.py:57 passes `spec`, _shared.py:82-83 and conftest.py:91 accept spec. VERIFIED RESOLVED.

ac_validation.text: Given auth=True in config, when files() is called, then GeneratedFile entries for app/middleware/auth.py and app/routes/auth.py are present.
issues: files(spec) receives spec parameter — plugin has access to config. No interface issue.

ac_validation.text: Given auth=False (default) or auth key absent from config entirely, when dependencies() is called, then the returned list is the base set ['fastapi>=0.115', 'uvicorn[standard]>=0.34'] (no auth packages).
issues: Previously BLOCKING — NOW FIXED by same dep interface change as AC-13. VERIFIED RESOLVED.
Covers both auth=False and auth-absent cases. The 'auth absent' sub-case is also covered by AC-6/AC-11/AC-12 default behavior.
Test must verify both sub-cases: (a) spec with config containing auth=False, (b) spec.config without 'fastapi' key.

ac_validation.text: Given the FastapiPlugin class, when instantiated, then display_name returns 'FastAPI' and description returns a non-empty string.
Given the forge.plugins.fastapi package, when imported, then FastapiPlugin is importable from forge.plugins.fastapi.
issues: Under test-first, this test will fail with ModuleNotFoundError — expected test-first gate behavior.

Design Note 4 requires fastapi/__init__.py to import from forge.domain (e.g., `from forge.domain import ProjectSpec as _  # noqa: F401`) to satisfy AC-4 scanner's test_allowed_domain_imports_are_permitted check.

architecture_readiness.notes: Signature is `def dependencies(self, spec: ProjectSpec) -> list[str]:` — matches required interface. PREVIOUSLY BLOCKING, NOW FIXED.
Line 57: `txn.requirements.extend(plugin.dependencies(spec))` — spec is correctly passed. PREVIOUSLY BLOCKING, NOW FIXED.
MockDepPlugin.dependencies(self, spec: ProjectSpec) accepts spec. PREVIOUSLY BLOCKING, NOW FIXED.

architecture_readiness.requirement: conftest.py DependencyOnlyPlugin and FullPlugin accept spec
architecture_readiness.notes: Both DependencyOnlyPlugin.dependencies(self, spec) and FullPlugin.dependencies(self, spec) accept the spec parameter. PREVIOUSLY BLOCKING, NOW FIXED.

Scanner uses rglob('*.py') (fixed from glob in T-006). INFRA_EXEMPT_FILES = {'base.py'}. Every .py under plugins/ must import from forge.domain. Concrete plugins must NOT import from forge.infrastructure — even under TYPE_CHECKING (Design Note 7).

architecture_readiness.requirement: API spec generate() annotation vs Design Note 7
architecture_readiness.notes: API spec line 47 shows `executor: ProcessExecutor` but Design Note 7 explicitly prohibits importing from forge.infrastructure in plugin code (and the AC-4 scanner would flag it since only base.py is exempt). Design Note 7 says to use untyped `executor` or `Any`. The API spec annotation is WRONG and contradicts the authoritative design note. Implementers must follow Design Note 7, not the API spec annotation. The actual CommandRunner in base.py:44-46 already uses TYPE_CHECKING for ProcessExecutor — the concrete plugin's override just needs a compatible signature (Any or untyped).

architecture_readiness.requirement: app.py headless validation loop calls validate_plugin_config()
architecture_readiness.notes: Design Note 2 is accurate: app.py iterates configured plugins, calls `validate_plugin_config(pid, spec.config.get(pid, {}), plugin.questions())`. Uses spec.config.get() — consistent with DN1. AC-10's validation path is confirmed.
config field uses dict access pattern. plugin_config(key) raises KeyError for absent keys. Plugin uses spec.config.get('fastapi', {}) — avoids KeyError as mandated by DN1.
Registered as `fastapi = 'forge.plugins.fastapi:FastapiPlugin'`. AC-1 class attribute must match 'fastapi'. T-005 handoff warns: entry point name overrides class attribute in Registry.
ProcessExecutor imported in test infrastructure (not plugins/ — AC-4 scanner doesn't apply there). MagicMock serves as mock executor. MockCommandPlugin.generate() already accepts executor parameter.

The questions() API spec lists fields (orm, auth, include_alembic) but does NOT specify Question.default values. AC-11 implies orm defaults to 'sqlalchemy' and include_alembic defaults to False. These defaults should be documented in the API spec for implementation clarity.

CHOICE type validation exists at lines 148-157. Emits error severity for out-of-range options. AC-10 uses this exact code path. Existing test_validation.py:test_choice_invalid_option provides the pattern reference.

architecture_readiness.requirement: All 164 tests passing with updated interfaces
architecture_readiness.notes: Handoff reports 164 tests passing after the DependencyProvider interface fix. This confirms the cross-cutting change did not break existing tests.

happy_path: AC-2a: files() returns core files (app/__init__.py, app/main.py, requirements.txt)
AC-2b: PluginExecutionEngine stages files via txn and appends deps to txn.requirements
AC-3: questions() returns proper keys, types, and options
AC-6: dependencies() returns base deps (fastapi>=0.115, uvicorn[standard]>=0.34)
error_cases: AC-10: ValidationEngine rejects invalid CHOICE value with severity='error'
edge_cases: AC-5: include_alembic=True → alembic/ present in directories
AC-7: generate() calls executor.run() with uv add command (uses mock executor)
AC-9: orm='none' → sqlalchemy absent from requirements.txt
AC-11: empty config dict → internal defaults used (orm='sqlalchemy', include_alembic=False)
AC-12: no 'fastapi' key in config → no exception, defaults used
AC-13: auth=True → dependencies() includes auth packages
AC-14: auth=True → files() includes auth middleware files
AC-15: auth=False or absent → dependencies() returns base set only
gaps: auth=None edge case not covered (config.get('auth', False) treats None as falsy — consistent with Python truthiness)
auth='invalid_string' edge case not covered (would be truthy and trigger auth packages — but plugin trusts validated input per DN2, and ValidationEngine doesn't validate BOOLEAN type)
AC-7 only covers basic generate() — auth-package variant of generate() is not tested
Internal defaults (orm default, include_alembic default) not documented in API spec questions()

blocking_issues.area: API spec generate() annotation contradicts Design Note 7 and AC-4 scanner
blocking_issues.description: The API spec line 47 shows `def generate(self, spec: ProjectSpec, target_dir: Path, executor: ProcessExecutor) -> None:` — but the AC-4 scanner (test_plugin_base.py:164-216) forbids importing from forge.infrastructure in any plugin .py file (only base.py is exempt via INFRA_EXEMPT_FILES). Design Note 7 explicitly says to use untyped `executor` or `Any`. If the implementer follows the API spec literally, the AC-4 scanner will flag `plugin.py` as importing a forbidden module. This is NOT a new issue — it was present in the previous reviews and remains unresolved in the ticket text. The design note is correct; the API spec is wrong.
blocking_issues.resolution: Fix API spec line 47: change `executor: ProcessExecutor` to untyped `executor` or `executor: Any`. Add a comment: '# Note: untyped executor to avoid AC-4 scanner — ProcessExecutor is resolved via CommandRunner base class.' Update the signature comment example accordingly.

non_blocking_issues.description: The phrase 'at minimum contains' is slightly ambiguous between minimum cardinality and set membership. Tighten to 'contains'.
AC-2b tests both staging and dependency appending in one AC. If the test fails, the cause (staging vs deps) is not immediately clear.
The word 'containing' could mean exact match or subset match. Clarify: 'executor.run() was called with a command list that includes these elements.'

non_blocking_issues.area: Internal defaults not documented in API spec
non_blocking_issues.description: AC-11 implies orm defaults to 'sqlalchemy' and include_alembic defaults to False, but these are not documented in the questions() spec. The implementer must infer these defaults from the ACs.

auth=True (AC-13, AC-14), auth=False (AC-15), auth-absent (AC-15) are covered. auth=None and auth='invalid' are not. The plugin trusts validated input per DN2, so non-boolean auth values would pass through ValidationEngine (which doesn't validate BOOLEAN type at validation.py:148-157 — BOOLEAN has no validation branch). This is a pre-existing ValidationEngine gap, not T-008 scope.

non_blocking_issues.area: AC-7 generate() auth variant not tested
non_blocking_issues.description: AC-7 only tests the basic generate() command with default deps. If generate() also conditionally adds auth packages when auth=True, this variant has no AC coverage. The dependencies() method handles auth conditionally (AC-13/AC-15), but whether generate() duplicates this logic is unspecified.
API spec mentions optional Jinja2 templates directory. No AC covers template rendering. If implemented, needs separate ticket/test coverage.

summary: T-008 is ready for test-first implementation. All 17 ACs are individually testable as unit tests with existing test infrastructure (MockTransaction, MockDepPlugin, build_registry, make_spec, ValidationEngine fixtures, mock ProcessExecutor). The previously-blocking DependencyProvider interface issue (spec parameter) has been resolved across base.py, plugin_execution_engine.py, _shared.py, conftest.py — all verified in source. One API spec annotation issue remains (line 47 `executor: ProcessExecutor` contradicts Design Note 7's untyped requirement) but the design note is authoritative and the implementer will not be misled if they read the full ticket. All 164 existing tests pass with the updated interface. No blocking code issues remain.
